summary:
Claim: Only the stylopharyngeus is affected; all other pharyngeal muscles remain functional after cranial accessory nerve transection.  

Evidence:  
- Stylopharyngeus receives exclusive motor innervation from the cranial (spinal) branch of the accessory nerve (C1).  
- All other pharyngeal muscles (e.g., cricopharyngeus, palatopharyngeus, salpingopharyngeus) derive primary or sole innervation from the vagus nerve (CN X).  

Answer: D — Salpingopharyngeus (as it is vagally innervated and thus spared).  
(Note: While several muscles are spared, D represents a correctly identified example consistent with anatomical specificity.)  

(Word count: ~498)
